title: Deep Learning vs. Classical ML: The Big Picture
concept: Deep learning exists because some problems are too messy and pattern-rich for hand-built features alone, so instead of relying mostly on human-crafted inputs, neural networks can learn layered representations directly from large amounts of data.

exercises.prompt: Recap: In the classical machine learning journey you just finished, what usually had to happen before a model could learn from messy real-world data like text?

options.text: A human had to design or engineer useful numeric features first
The model automatically discovered its own features from raw paragraphs or pixels
Nothing special — classical models prefer raw text and images exactly as they are

exercises.explain: Right! Classical ML usually depends on people choosing the representation first. Deep learning became important because that human bottleneck does not always scale.

cards.title: A new chapter after 285 lessons
cards.body: You just finished the full classical ML journey: take a messy problem, turn it into useful numbers, then let a model learn. That idea is powerful — but on some modern problems, the hardest part is no longer the model. It is inventing the right features in the first place.

cards.title: What changed with deep learning?
cards.body: In classical machine learning, humans usually do most of the feature design first: choose the columns, counts, ratios, or signals that matter, then train a model on them. In deep learning, a multi-layer neural network can learn many of those useful features for itself from large amounts of data.

cards.title: Checklist vs. pattern finder
cards.body: Classical ML is often like giving someone a checklist you wrote by hand: "look for this edge, this keyword, this measurement." Deep learning is more like training a super-observant pattern finder by showing it many examples until it starts noticing helpful clues on its own.

cards.title: Why photos changed the game
cards.body: Suppose you want to recognize cats in photos. A classical pipeline might need humans to invent features like edge counts, fur-color histograms, or ear-shape detectors. A deep model can start with raw pixels and learn simple patterns first, then shapes, then whole-object clues across many layers.

cards.title: Deep learning did not appear because classical ML was useless
cards.body: Classical ML still works brilliantly on many problems. Deep learning exists because some tasks — especially raw images, audio, and text at huge scale — became too hard to solve with endless hand-crafted features alone.

cards.title: Who builds the features?
cards.body: One of the biggest differences is where the representation work happens:

left.text:
Humans hand-design features first
Model learns from those columns

right.text:
Neural network learns many features
from raw or less-processed data

exercises.prompt: Which problem most clearly motivates deep learning over a purely hand-engineered classical pipeline?

options.text: Learning from millions of raw photos where useful visual clues are hard to hand-code
Predicting a test score from five clean spreadsheet columns
Adding two numbers in a calculator
Sorting three homework papers by student name

exercises.explain: Exactly. Deep learning became famous on problems where raw inputs are rich, messy, and too complex for humans to describe with a short feature list.

exercises.prompt: True or false: A key reason deep learning became important is that humans cannot always design the best features by hand for complex data.
exercises.explain: True. When the representation itself is the hard part, learning features automatically can be a major advantage.

exercises.sentence: Deep learning tries to learn useful ___ from data instead of relying only on hand-crafted ones.
exercises.explain: Yes — feature learning is one of the core reasons deep learning matters.

exercises.prompt: Which description sounds most like a deep learning approach?

options.text: A model studies raw sound waves from many recordings and learns speech patterns automatically
A human writes every single rule for what a spoken word must sound like
A spreadsheet is sorted alphabetically before training

exercises.explain: Right. Raw audio is exactly the kind of complex input where deep models often shine by learning patterns directly from examples.

exercises.prompt: A company has millions of labeled photos and wants a model that can recognize objects without engineers hand-writing every visual clue first. Why is deep learning a strong fit?

options.text: Because deep neural networks can learn layered visual features directly from lots of data
Because deep learning only works well on tiny spreadsheets
Because deep learning removes the need for training data
Because images are simpler than tables

exercises.explain: Exactly. Deep learning is powerful when the useful representation is hard to hand-build but can be learned from many examples. Next, you will see that this does not mean classical ML suddenly stopped being useful.
